stories.title: Israel's war strategy involves high-level assassinations. But will it win the war?
stories.description: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.

stories.title: The effects of the Iran war on environmental and human health, according to an expert
stories.description: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants as war enters fourth week with no end in sight
stories.description: We have the latest on the U-S and Israeli war on Iran, where in the past 48 hours, Israel has struck one of Iran's nuclear facilities and Iran has responded with strikes in Israel.

stories.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants as Iran strikes 2 Israeli cities
stories.description: Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why there's no sign of the conflict in the Middle East 'winding down'
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister said that it doesn't appear that the US is prepared to halt its aggression after President Trump said the US was considering "winding down" its war.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
stories.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

stories.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
stories.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
stories.description: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

stories.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
stories.description: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Live: Iran faces Trump's Hormuz ultimatum as IEA warns of worst global energy crisis in decades
stories.description: Iran on Monday faces a deadline set by US President Donald Trump to open up the crucial Strait of Hormuz or face a major US assault on power plants, as Israel warned of weeks more of war. The International Energy Agency said the crisis is more severe than the 1970s oil shocks com...

stories.title: Iran threatens to cripple Gulf water, energy systems after Trump ultimatum
stories.description: Iran said on Sunday it would target Gulf energy and water infrastructure if US President Donald Trump follows through on a threat to strike its power grid within 48 hours, escalating the conflict. Air raid sirens sounded across Israel after overnight Iranian missile attacks injur...

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: Trump’s ultimatum to Iran
stories.description: Donald #Trump issued an ultimatum to #Iran on March 22: 48 hours to reopen the Strait of #Hormuz or the #US will strike Iranian oil facilities. Tehran has responded, warning it would retaliate.

stories.title: A former senior advisor to the Saudi government assesses the likelihood of a Gulf-style NATO
stories.description: Gulf states are caught between a rock and a hard place, right now as the consider how to respond to the Iran's regime, dealing with a volley of attacks on critical infrastrure, and threats that continue.

stories.title: Trump threat to hit Iran power plants could violate Geneva Conventions
stories.description: US President Donald Trump threatened overnight to “obliterate” Iran’s power plants if Tehran does not fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours, barely a day after suggesting he was “winding down” the war. Striking Iran’s energy sites, including the Bushehr nuclear power ...

stories.title: Nabatieh under fire: Life inside a war zone in southern Lebanon
stories.description: In the southern Lebanese city of Nabatieh, paramedics and residents are living under near-constant air strikes, as Israel targets areas it says are linked to Hezbollah. With much of the population displaced, those who remain face daily danger, limited supplies, and relentless unc...

stories.title: China ‘is playing the long game’ in Iran war by staying neutral
stories.description: Han Shen Lin, director of the China Office at the Asia Consultative Group, says that China is likely to remain neutral,

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 24 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: The region is on edge as Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face attacks on power plants nears.

stories.title: IEA chief warns of ‘very severe’ global energy crisis
stories.description: IEA may release more oil as the Iran war hits supply, with chief Fatih Birol warning of a severe global energy crisis.

stories.title: World in energy crisis worse than 1970s’ oil shocks combined, IEA head says
stories.description: Energy crunch prompted by Iran war poses 'major threat' to global economy, Fatih Birol says.

stories.title: Brothers search rubble for missing sibling under Tehran rubble after strike
stories.description: Five days after a strike destroyed a building in Tehran, Mahdi Mirzahosseini’s brothers are still searching for him.

stories.title: Immense damage seen in Iran’s streets after air strikes
stories.description: Videos show the aftermath of strikes in Iran, as search teams recover bodies from rubble.

stories.title: Asian stock markets plunge amid Trump’s ultimatum on Iran
stories.description: Key indexes in Japan, South Korea and Hong Kong tumble as Iran threatens attacks on energy infrastructure across region.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran vows to ‘completely close’ Hormuz if power plants hit
stories.description: US-Israel attacks on Iran continue as Israeli forces blow up the Qasimiyah Bridge in south Lebanon.

stories.title: Kashmiris donate gold and cash to support Iran
stories.description: Residents in Indian-administered Kashmir are donating their gold possessions and cash to support Iranians.

stories.title: Who’s left running Iran?
stories.description: Much of the top leadership has been eliminated, but the country is still fighting.

stories.title: Tehran refuses to back down as Hormuz remains global pain point

stories.title: Fresh push in Congress to curb Trump’s war powers
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Fresh efforts are under way in both the House and the Senate to rein in President Donald Trump’s authority to wage war against Iran, as Democratic leaders renewed their push to force a vote on ending US involvement in hostilities not authorised by Congress. In the Hou...

stories.title: China pledges more balanced trade and further opening of the economy after record surplus
stories.description: Chinese Premier Li Qiang pledged on Sunday to further open the country’s economy to foreign firms and pursue more balanced trade with its global partners, after a year marked by trade friction and tariff wars with the United States and European Union in particular. China will imp...

stories.title: European nations decry ‘increasing settler terror’ in occupied West Bank
stories.description: Diplomats from 13 European countries and Canada on Saturday slammed growing “terror” by settlers against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, after a surge in deadly attacks. Since the start of March, six Palestinians have been shot dead in settler attacks in the West Bank, ac...

stories.title: Iran missiles hit southern Israel, injuring more than 100
stories.description: Two Iranian missiles struck southern Israel on Saturday, injuring more than 100 people in the most destructive attack of the three-week war, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowing to retaliate “on all fronts”. The strikes tore open the facades of residential buildings and ...

stories.title: Situation in Strait of Hormuz to normalize when conflict over Iran ends — Russian MFA
stories.description: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Andrey Rudenko said Everything that is happening in the Strait of Hormuz is a direct consequence of this illegal campaign

stories.title: Iran accuses US of hiding true death toll from aggression against republic
stories.description: According to the spokesman for the Iranian Khatam-al Anbiya headquarters, Washington is staging a media circus

stories.title: More US troops dissatisfied with prospects of war with Iran — HuffPost
stories.description: Military personnel, in particular, believe that a poorly planned operation exposes them to unnecessary danger without any visible strategic benefit

stories.title: US has no good options regarding Iran — The Economist
stories.description: To almost everyone else it is clear that America entered this war with a "flawed strategy," the article said

stories.description: The police also said that the identification and arrest of agents and traitors is ongoing

stories.title: One person killed in attack on Radio Center in Bandar Abbas, southern Iran
stories.description: Another person was injured, Iranian state television reported

stories.title: Iran to respond asymmetrically to threats against energy infrastructure
stories.description: On Saturday, US President Donald Trump said that the United States would destroy a number of Iran’s power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not fully opened for shipping within 48 hours

stories.description: The UAE Defense Ministry has not yet provided information on the effectiveness of repelling the Iranian attack

stories.description: According to the news portal, such an incident has been recorded for the first time since the start of Israel’s operation against Iran

stories.description: The Iranian army said earlier in the day that an F-15 fighter jet had been attacked and shot down near Hormuz Island off Iran’s southern coast

stories.title: AfD leaders calls for ending conflict in Ukraine through diplomacy
stories.description: According to the AfD leader, a realistic variant of a peace agreement should include security guarantees for both parties, as well as the lifting of sanctions, and the establishment of an international fund to restore territories affected by the conflict

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz closed only for those who violate Iran’s borders — president
stories.description: The illusion of erasing Iran from the map shows desperation against the will of a history-making nation, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
